reasoning: Saint-Barthélemy is a tiny Caribbean island with a very small population (under 11,000). The ACLED dataset records 0 protests over the past 360 days (reference value of 1.000 means 30-day average was 0, so 1 + 0 = 1). The target requires more than ten times this base value (10 * 1 = 10) in a single 30-day window, meaning 11 or more protests. In a place this small, 11+ protests in a month would require an extraordinary and highly disruptive, continuous wave of demonstrations. Extensive searches across English and French news sources revealed no evidence of any protests occurring in or planned for Saint-Barthélemy in early 2026. Given the extremely low base rate and complete lack of current evidence for any unrest, the probability of hitting 11+ protests in the 30 days prior to Feb 22 or March 17 is vanishingly small. I am reporting 2% for both dates rather than 0% because 2% is the minimum allowed, though practically it is functionally zero.
